messages.settings-introduction: Choose how WyrmGrid presents information without changing its source facts.
messages.settings-atlas-detail: Keep your synchronization interval and layer visibility consistent between launches.
messages.privacy-atlas-restore-view-detail: Store the last map centre, zoom, bearing, and pitch in this device's encrypted database. Turning this off deletes the saved view.
messages.privacy-atlas-boundary: Layer visibility is remembered automatically. Last-view restoration is off by default and never sends your map position to a plugin.
messages.settings-units-detail: Choose each measurement category independently, or use a preset as a starting point.
messages.settings-units-boundary: These choices affect WyrmGrid presentation only. They do not modify simulator settings, aircraft state, fuel load, canonical snapshots, or plugin facts.
messages.settings-accessibility-detail: Choose whether information surfaces react gently to pointer movement and keyboard focus.
messages.settings-responsive-surfaces-detail: Allow cards and text panels to shift and glow subtly as you explore them. No information depends on the effect.
messages.settings-motion-boundary: Your operating system's Reduce Motion preference always takes priority. Turning this off removes the movement while preserving the same content and focus cues.
messages.settings-weather-graphics-detail: Choose the GPU detail level and which sourced weather phenomena Atlas may visualise.
messages.settings-weather-profile-compatibility-d: Conservative static markers with the lowest graphics cost.
messages.settings-weather-profile-enhanced-detail: GPU atmosphere, wind vectors, and restrained motion with a balanced graphics budget.
messages.settings-weather-profile-cinematic-detai: Layered cloud depth, denser visible precipitation, lightning illumination, and dust volumes for capable GPUs.
messages.settings-weather-clouds-detail: Layer cloud shading only where a sourced grid or explicit station condition supports it.
messages.settings-weather-precipitation-detail: Show animated rain and snow marks driven by explicit precipitation facts.
messages.settings-weather-lightning-detail: Show storm-cell lightning symbols and illumination only for explicit convective conditions.
messages.settings-weather-dust-detail: Render layered airborne dust around stations reporting dust, sand, or dust-storm codes.
messages.settings-weather-reduce-flashes-detail: Keep Cinematic lightning illumination steady and subdued. Enabled by default and always enforced by your operating system's Reduce Motion setting.
messages.settings-weather-flash-confirmation: Turning off reduced weather flashes allows brief Cinematic lightning illumination. This may affect people with photosensitive epilepsy. Continue?
messages.settings-weather-boundary: Graphics never create weather coverage, cloud altitude, movement, or strike locations that the source did not provide. Compatibility and low-resource overrides preserve the same facts with simpler visuals.
messages.atlas-radar-static-mode: Newest frame · static for motion or performance preference
messages.atlas-radar-no-data-key: Grey areas have no RADAR coverage
messages.atlas-radar-coverage-unknown: Coverage mask unavailable · transparency is not clear sky
messages.dispatch-route-weather-intro: Indicative model conditions sampled about every { $interval } nm and matched to planned arrival times when forecast data permits. This is broad context, not a flight-safety assessment.
messages.dispatch-route-weather-timing-departure-: A planned departure time was not supplied. Samples are current context only and are not ETA-matched forecasts.
messages.dispatch-route-weather-timing-duration-u: A usable planned enroute duration was not supplied. Samples are current context only and are not ETA-matched forecasts.
messages.dispatch-route-weather-current-context: Current model context · not matched to ETA
messages.dispatch-route-weather-no-layer-title: No global model layer is available.
messages.dispatch-route-weather-no-layer-detail: Enable and refresh a compatible global-weather plugin to add along-route context.
messages.dispatch-route-weather-radar-detail: RADAR is current or past evidence only. WyrmGrid does not extrapolate it to your planned arrival times.
messages.atlas-weather-status-detail-starting: MapLibre effects remain visible while Three.js starts
messages.atlas-weather-status-detail-fallback: MapLibre effects retained · detailed renderer unavailable
messages.atlas-weather-status-source-boundary: Source-shaped only · station effects remain METAR-local
messages.settings-simulator-detail: Choose the trusted sidecar WyrmGrid should use for simulator telemetry.
messages.settings-simulator-auto-start-detail: When enabled, WyrmGrid launches the provider in the background. It can wait harmlessly for the simulator.
messages.settings-recording-auto-start-detail: Off by default. Two direct airborne observations start a recording; WyrmGrid never guesses from altitude alone.
messages.settings-recording-auto-stop-detail: Only recordings started automatically are stopped by lifecycle evidence. Manual recordings remain under your control.
messages.settings-simulator-boundary: Provider launch, automatic recording, and retention are separate. Automatic recording is off until you enable it. Recordings remain local and are not granted to community plugins.
messages.settings-theme-detail: Choose or import the application colour theme.
messages.settings-language-detail: Choose or import a community language pack.
messages.settings-privacy-detail: Review legal documents and optional diagnostics.
messages.security-settings-link-detail: Review active access, consent status, and recent authorization decisions.
messages.data-protection-settings-link-detail: Review local encryption, create a portable backup, or prepare a restore.
messages.data-protection-licences-settings-detail: Review notices for SQLCipher and other bundled security components.
messages.security-introduction: See which actors can access WyrmGrid capabilities and review the decisions recorded on this device.
messages.security-boundary-detail: This view is read from WyrmGrid's internal authorization service. Interface controls do not create authority, and the audit trail stores symbolic decisions rather than credentials or source data.
messages.security-active-actors-detail: Actors with one or more current capabilities
messages.security-legal-detail: Current document acknowledgement on this device
messages.security-status-unavailable: The local authorization record is unavailable.
messages.security-no-active-access-detail: Installed actors remain denied until you approve their exact version and requested capability set.
messages.security-no-history: No authorization decisions have been recorded yet.
messages.forge-start-with-wyrmgrid-detail: Remember this choice and start the plugin on future launches. Stopping it manually affects only the current session.
messages.forge-start-with-wyrmgrid-standing-requi: Standing access is required so WyrmGrid can safely start this plugin on a future launch.
messages.forge-host-settings-detail: WyrmGrid owns and validates these non-secret choices. The plugin cannot change them itself.
messages.forge-setting-forecast-refresh-detail: How often WyrmGrid asks this plugin for a new model-weather layer.
messages.forge-setting-radar-refresh-detail: How often WyrmGrid asks this plugin for a new received RADAR frame.
messages.forge-setting-unknown-detail: This setting needs a newer WyrmGrid interface before it can be described here.
messages.data-protection-introduction: Protect local WyrmGrid data and carry it safely to another installation without exposing the device key.
messages.data-protection-database-detail: SQLCipher protects the database file while WyrmGrid is closed.
messages.data-protection-device-key-detail: Held by the operating system credential store, separately from the database.
messages.data-protection-pending-detail: Restart WyrmGrid to validate and activate the selected backup. The current database is retained for rollback until activation succeeds.
messages.data-protection-backup-detail: Choose a new file and a dedicated password. The backup contains your retained WyrmGrid data, including Hoard history and local preferences.
messages.data-protection-password-warning: WyrmGrid does not store or recover this password. Keep it in a trusted password manager before relying on the backup.
messages.data-protection-restore-detail: WyrmGrid validates the backup now, then replaces local data only after the next launch succeeds.
messages.data-protection-restore-confirm: I understand that restarting WyrmGrid will replace the local database with this backup.
messages.data-protection-restore-prepared: Restore validated and prepared. Restart WyrmGrid when you are ready to activate it.
messages.data-protection-reset-detail: This schedules the active encrypted SQLite database for deletion and immediately restarts WyrmGrid with a new, empty database. This cannot be undone without a portable backup.
messages.data-protection-reset-erases-history: OnAir snapshots, Hoard history, flight operations, and remembered provider identifiers.
messages.data-protection-reset-erases-recordings: Simulator recordings, samples, events, evidence, and recording preferences.
messages.data-protection-reset-erases-preferences: Themes, language packs, units, display, synchronization, Atlas layers and last view, weather, simulator, plugin host settings, and automatic-start preferences.
messages.data-protection-reset-erases-access: Plugin permissions and startup choices, authorization history, legal acknowledgements, and telemetry consent.
messages.data-protection-reset-keeps-detail: Portable backup files, installed plugin files, diagnostics, simulator sidecars, the browser webview itself, or credentials held separately by the operating system. Use Security & permissions to forget the separately stored OnAir API key.
messages.data-protection-reset-acknowledge: I understand this deletes every record and preference in the local database and that WyrmGrid will restart immediately.
messages.data-protection-licences-detail: WyrmGrid includes open-source cryptographic and storage components under their respective licences.
messages.data-protection-sqlcipher-notice: WyrmGrid uses SQLCipher Community Edition to encrypt its SQLite database and portable backups.
messages.data-protection-openssl-notice: The bundled SQLCipher build uses OpenSSL. Its complete licence is available from the OpenSSL project and must accompany WyrmGrid release materials.
messages.error-data-protection-password-too-short: Use at least 12 characters for the portable-backup password.
messages.error-data-protection-password-too-long: The portable-backup password is too long.
messages.error-data-protection-password-mismatch: The two portable-backup passwords do not match.
messages.error-data-protection-restore-confirmati: Confirm that restoring will replace current local data after WyrmGrid restarts.
messages.error-data-protection-reset-confirmation: Type the displayed confirmation phrase exactly to confirm permanent local-data deletion.
messages.error-data-protection-destination-exists: Choose a new filename; WyrmGrid will not overwrite an existing backup.
messages.error-data-protection-invalid-backup: That file is not a valid WyrmGrid portable backup, is damaged, or the password is wrong.
messages.error-data-protection-active-database-se: Choose a portable backup rather than WyrmGrid's active database.
messages.error-data-protection-persistent-storage: Portable backup and restore require persistent encrypted storage.
messages.error-data-protection-storage-unavailabl: WyrmGrid could not complete the encrypted data operation.
messages.error-plugin-standing-permission-require: Choose standing access before enabling automatic plugin startup.
messages.error-plugin-unknown-configuration: That plugin setting is not available in this WyrmGrid version.
messages.error-plugin-invalid-configuration: Choose one of the available values for that plugin setting.
messages.error-atlas-preferences-storage-unavaila: WyrmGrid could not read or save its encrypted Atlas settings.
messages.error-atlas-invalid-preference: That Atlas preference is outside the supported range.
messages.telemetry-simulator-introduction: Start a trusted provider sidecar to bring bounded, read-only simulator facts into WyrmGrid.
messages.telemetry-simulator-boundary-detail: SimConnect remains outside the desktop process. Community plugins receive this sanitized snapshot only after simulator telemetry permission is approved.
messages.simulator-no-providers-detail: Bundled and approved community provider sidecars will appear here.
messages.simulator-recording-detail: Recording stores bounded one-hertz simulator facts on this device. Completed sessions are removed after { $days } days unless pinned or deleted sooner.
messages.simulator-recording-empty: No simulator recordings yet. Connect to a live flight, then start recording when you are ready.
messages.simulator-recording-delete-confirm: Delete this local simulator recording? This cannot be undone.
messages.simulator-recording-delete-all-confirm: Delete every completed local simulator recording? This cannot be undone.
messages.simulator-recording-aircraft-changed: Recording stopped because the provider reported a different aircraft. WyrmGrid did not join the two flights.
messages.simulator-recording-storage-failed: Recording stopped because local storage became unavailable.
messages.simulator-recording-window: Exact samples { $first }–{ $last } from the newest end
messages.simulator-recording-exact-window-detail: Use this paged view when individual retained samples matter. The debrief above covers the whole recording.
messages.simulator-debrief-summary: A bounded view of all { $samples } retained samples. Extremes and observed gaps are preserved when a long recording is reduced for display.
messages.simulator-debrief-open-atlas: Open plan and flight in Atlas
messages.simulator-debrief-unresolved-route: { $count } planned route points lack coordinates and are not plotted:
messages.simulator-recording-comparison-boundary: SimBrief facts stay attributed; missing values remain unavailable.
messages.simulator-recording-comparison-takeoff-f: Planned take-off / recorded start fuel weight
messages.simulator-recording-comparison-landing-f: Planned landing / recorded end fuel weight
messages.simulator-recording-analysis-withheld: Analysis is withheld because this recording exceeds the current exact-analysis bound. The recording itself remains intact.
messages.simulator-recording-events-empty: No lifecycle events were recorded for this flight.
messages.hoard-recording-detail: Review retained telemetry sessions and unit-aware graphs. Completed recordings remain on this device for { $days } days unless deleted sooner.
messages.hoard-recording-empty: No simulator recordings are stored in Hoard yet. Start one from the Simulator bridge while a flight is live.
messages.simulator-detail-starting: Launching the provider and verifying its Bridge identity.
messages.simulator-detail-stopped: The provider is stopped. Start it when you want WyrmGrid to read simulator telemetry.
messages.simulator-detail-unsupported-platform: This provider does not support the current operating system or architecture.
messages.simulator-detail-waiting: The provider is ready and waiting for an active MSFS 2024 flight.
messages.simulator-detail-connected: The read-only telemetry channel is connected.
messages.simulator-detail-disconnected: The simulator connection ended. Live telemetry has been withheld.
messages.simulator-detail-stale: No fresh simulator sample has arrived for { $seconds } seconds. The previous snapshot is hidden until the stream recovers.
messages.simulator-detail-status-update: The provider reported a status update.
messages.error-simulator-provider-executable-unav: The simulator connector is missing. Restart a development launch to prepare it automatically; an installed build may need repair.
messages.error-simconnect-client-unavailable: The provider could not load the MSFS 2024 SimConnect client.
messages.error-simulator-provider-handshake: The provider did not complete its trusted Bridge handshake.
messages.error-simulator-provider-protocol: The provider sent an invalid or incompatible Bridge message.
messages.error-simulator-provider-connection: The provider process closed its Bridge connection unexpectedly.
messages.jobs-introduction: Review pending company jobs as read-only OnAir facts, then carry one into Dispatch for plan comparison.
messages.jobs-empty-detail: Connect and synchronize OnAir to populate this workspace.
messages.jobs-no-description: OnAir did not report a description for this job.
messages.jobs-read-only-note: WyrmGrid does not accept, modify, or complete OnAir jobs. Dispatch only compares this retained observation with an imported plan.
messages.jobs-awaiting-detail: The Jobs workspace will appear here after a successful read-only synchronization.
messages.jobs-read-only-banner: Pending jobs are observed from OnAir and never accepted or changed by WyrmGrid
messages.theme-introduction: Themes can change WyrmGrid's approved colour roles. They cannot run code, load remote assets, move controls, or read application data.
messages.theme-community-files-detail: Versioned JSON, validated locally before it is saved.
messages.theme-import-desktop-only: Theme import is available in the desktop application
messages.language-introduction: English (Australia) is the canonical source. Community packs may translate ordinary interface messages and always fall back to English when a message is missing.
messages.language-community-files-detail: Versioned JSON and Fluent patterns, validated locally before they are saved.
messages.language-import-desktop-only: Language-pack import is available in the desktop application
messages.security-language-boundary: Unreviewed community packs cannot replace legal, credential, telemetry, plugin-permission, destructive-action, or diagnostic messages.
messages.dispatch-finding-onair-fleet-unavailable: Connect or synchronize OnAir to compare this plan with an observed company aircraft.
messages.dispatch-finding-payload-unavailable-exp: The current OnAir fleet contract does not include job payload or aircraft weight limits, so WyrmGrid does not infer compatibility.
messages.dispatch-finding-deadline-unavailable-ex: The current OnAir slice does not include job schedules or deadlines. Planned block times remain visible without an OnAir deadline verdict.
messages.dispatch-finding-job-unselected-explanat: Choose a pending job to compare its route, payload, and expiry with this plan.
messages.dispatch-finding-job-route-match-explana: The SimBrief endpoints match the first departure and final destination in the selected job.
messages.dispatch-finding-job-route-difference-ex: The plan endpoints do not match the selected job.
messages.dispatch-finding-job-route-unavailable-e: The selected job did not report both endpoint ICAOs.
messages.dispatch-finding-job-payload-match-expla: The SimBrief payload matches the selected job cargo weight. Aircraft capacity is not inferred.
messages.dispatch-finding-job-payload-difference-: The SimBrief payload differs from the selected job cargo weight. Aircraft capacity is not inferred.
messages.dispatch-finding-job-payload-unavailable: SimBrief or the selected job did not report a cargo value that can be compared.
messages.dispatch-finding-job-deadline-match-expl: The imported plan arrives before the selected job expires. This is not a completion guarantee.
messages.dispatch-finding-job-deadline-missed-exp: The imported plan arrives after the selected job expires.
messages.dispatch-finding-job-deadline-unavailabl: The imported plan or selected job did not report the required schedule value.
messages.dispatch-finding-registration-match-expl: The SimBrief registration exactly matches one aircraft in the observed OnAir fleet.
messages.dispatch-finding-registration-not-found-: No aircraft in the observed OnAir fleet has the SimBrief registration.
messages.dispatch-finding-registration-ambiguous-: More than one observed aircraft uses this registration, so WyrmGrid will not choose one.
messages.dispatch-finding-model-candidate-explana: One OnAir aircraft has the exact model label, but a model match does not prove it is the same airframe.
messages.dispatch-finding-airframe-unmatched-expl: The plan has no registration and its model label does not identify exactly one OnAir aircraft.
messages.dispatch-finding-plan-aircraft-missing-e: SimBrief supplied neither a registration nor a model that can be compared deterministically.
messages.dispatch-finding-model-match-explanation: SimBrief and OnAir supplied the same normalized model label.
messages.dispatch-finding-model-difference-explan: The source labels differ. WyrmGrid has not used a type catalogue to declare them compatible or incompatible.
messages.dispatch-finding-aircraft-vocabularies-e: SimBrief supplied an ICAO type while OnAir supplied a model label; no unverified crosswalk was applied.
messages.dispatch-finding-model-unavailable-expla: One or both sources did not provide comparable aircraft model text.
messages.dispatch-finding-position-match-explanat: The matched OnAir aircraft is currently observed at the planned departure airport.
messages.dispatch-finding-position-difference-exp: The matched OnAir aircraft is observed at a different airport; positioning may be required.
messages.dispatch-finding-position-unavailable-ex: No deterministically matched aircraft with a current OnAir airport is available.
messages.error-language-storage-unavailable: WyrmGrid could not read or save its local language settings.
messages.error-language-import-failed: WyrmGrid could not import that language pack.
messages.error-language-apply-failed: WyrmGrid could not apply that language pack.
messages.error-onair-rate-limited: OnAir is rate-limiting requests. Wait before trying again.
